steps.title: Set up the angle relationship
steps.content: Lines $\ell$ and $k$ are parallel; transversal $t$ creates co-interior (same-side interior) angles $x$ and $y$. By the Co-Interior Angle Theorem these are supplementary: $$x + y = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• "$y \lt 15$": Because $y = 180 - x$ and $x \gt 165$, we get $y \lt 15$. ✓
  • "$y \gt 15$": This requires $180 - x \gt 15$, i.e., $x \lt 165$. That contradicts $x \gt 165$. ✗
  • "$x + y \lt 180$": Co-interior angles on parallel lines sum to exactly 180, never less. ✗
  • "$x + y \gt 180$": Same reason; the sum is always exactly 180. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
